channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: EVs become flashpoint in pivotal Michigan Senate race
item.description: Electric vehicles (EVs) are emerging as a flashpoint in Michigan’s pivotal Senate race. Republican former Rep. Mike Rogers (Mich.) has put Democratic Rep. Elissa Slotkin (Mich.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Stanford researchers map ‘White-Only’ properties using AI
item.description: Racially restrictive covenants are clauses in property deeds that ban individuals of a particular race from buying a home.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Miss having a dedicated work phone? Here’s how to configure your iPhone to separate business from personal use
item.description: Smartphones have enabled us to attend to work from nearly anywhere. But their productivity power also frequently encroaches on our personal lives—making it too easy to reply to business emails or Slack messages when we should be focused on dinner with the fam.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Rising crypto scams leave Americans reeling from billions in losses
item.description: Scammers have illustrated that cryptocurrency is not as foolproof as it has promised. Americans lost $5.6 billion to crypto scams in 2023, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two Virginia congressional candidates debated an AI chatbot. It was shockingly rational
item.description: On Thursday night an AI chatbot participated in a political debate with candidates for a congressional seat, and, despite a few technical glitches, performed well. There was no robot voice, no off-topic tangents, no personal attacks, no odd “they’re eating the dogs” hallucinations.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: DoD ‘fine tunes’ final CMMC program rule, industry turns attention to implementation
item.description: The Pentagon made some key tweaks to the final CMMC program rule. Several program watchers said the changes should help simplify a complex compliance program. The post DoD ‘fine tunes’ final CMMC program rule, industry turns attention to implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: A federal judge has delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Recent recalls raise concerns about food safety, but experts credit better regulation and technology
item.description: According to FDA data, nearly 1,200 food products have been recalled so far in 2024. Two hundred products were recalled in August, the highest monthly total this year.
item.dc:creator: Vivian La, The Mercury News

item.title: Right-wing influencers hyped anti-Ukraine videos made by a TV producer also funded by Russian media
item.description: It's a trend that concerns current and former U.S. intelligence officials and disinformation experts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: States grasping the benefits of, need for cyber harmonization
item.description: Public and private sector experts say programs like StateRAMP make their data and networks safer as cyber threats become more serious and resources sparse. The post States grasping the benefits of, need for cyber harmonization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Tesla software under investigation after collisions
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) opened an investigation into Tesla’s Full Self-Driving (FSD) software on Thursday after the system was reportedly involved in four crashes, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Data center provider fakes Tier 4 data center certificate to bag $11M SEC deal
item.description: Deepak Jain, CEO of a Maryland-based IT services firm, has been indicted for fraud and making false statements after allegedly falsifying a Tier 4 data center certification to secure a $10.7 million contract with the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: One Direction was the internet's first boy band, and Liam Payne its grounding force
item.description: One Direction member Liam Payne was found dead this week after falling from a hotel balcony in Buenos Aires, Argentina, at just 31.
item.dc:creator: Maria Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How you can help protect your aging parents from scammers
item.description: "They were raised to be a very trusting generation, a very rule following generation, so they make the assumption that everyone has their best interest at heart," Paula Daigle said.
item.dc:creator: Hayley Wielgus, NewsNation

item.title: Alabama man arrested for hacking Securities and Exchange Commission X account
item.description: Following the unauthorized announcement made during the hack, the DOJ said the price of bitcoin increased by more than $1,000 per bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Logan Sparkman, NewsNation

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Boeing competitor Airbus to cut up to 2,500 jobs in space, defense sector
item.description: Boeing competitor Airbus on Wednesday announced it would take steps to cut 2,500 jobs in the company’s defense and space division through the middle of 2026.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Run a business? Then you need to join this Apple service
item.description: Apple just gave smaller enterprises a chance to compete on a more equal basis with larger concerns with three nice improvements to Apple Business Connect: Business Caller ID, brand identity in Mail, and custom business logos for use with Tap to Pay.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
